mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median forward returns over 60 trading days in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is declining over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic expansion and supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns across all market regimes with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently coincide with positive 20-day equity returns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly consumption expenditures signals shifting demand composition that favors equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle regimes are followed by positive median 20-day returns over a 5-year backtest period.
